title: Fixing flood dynamic path discovery for dm

messages.author: Maveric
messages.text: Currently when it’s a large network with many hops or using a lora mode which has long tx time. It’s possible to retry flood messages when the ack(s) may be in transit back to the source of the dm. What happens is that the first ack lands and the path is learned and set. But then the second flood retry message lands and the destination sends another ack back to the source. When this ack lands the first path is overridden and now the learned path may be sub optimal. The client app should store both paths when the acks received were for the same message. It should leave the first path. But if the first dm on routed path times out just try the second path and alternate paths if there are timeouts and retries. The airtime and rfspace was already used to find these paths we shouldn’t drop them. We should then start a counter for which path is more successful and that path becomes primary path

But then the second flood retry message lands and the destination sends another ack back to the source. When this ack lands the first path is overridden and now the learned path may be sub optimal. The client app should store both paths when the acks received were for the same message. It should leave the first path. But if the first dm on routed path times out just try the second path and alternate paths if there are timeouts and retries. The airtime and rfspace was already used to find these paths we shouldn’t drop them. We should then start a counter for which path is more successful and that path becomes primary path
messages.reactions_html: 6

messages.author: Maveric
messages.content_html: @Liam Cottle
